revision.comment: だれかがシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外人間が作ったものは見当たらない。
revision.text: '''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、大雪山中のお花畑の中に湧く秘湯。所在は[[北海道]][[上川郡]][東川町]]。 ==アクセス== 旭川市街からバスで[[旭岳温泉]]まで約100分、そこからロープウェイに乗って姿見の池に到着、その後徒歩約2時間。このアクセス道は秀麗な旭岳を右手に見ながら、チングルマの咲き誇るお花畑の中を歩く。途中はぬかるみが多く登山靴の使用をおすすめする。また高山なので雨具は必携。愛山渓温泉へ行く分岐点（裾合平）から右に少し登った渓谷沿いにある。 ==泉質== 硫黄臭がする。泉温はかなり高くそのままでは熱すぎて入れないので、雪渓から流れ出ている渓流水を引き込んで調節する。この温度調整はシャベルまたは手により、湯船に流れ込む水路を土木工事して実施する。なお入湯後は肌がすべすべになる。 ==温泉街== だれかがシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外人間が作ったものは見当たらない。本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし長湯は禁物で付近には虻がたくさんいて裸の人間を見ると寄ってくる。

'''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、[[大雪山]]中のお花畑の中に湧く秘湯。所在は[[北海道]][[上川郡]][[東川町]]。 ==アクセス== 旭川市街からバスで[[旭岳温泉]]まで約100分、そこから[[ロープウェイ]]に乗って姿見の池に到着、その後徒歩約2時間。このアクセス道は秀麗な[[旭岳]]を右手に見ながら、[[チングルマ]]の咲き誇るお花畑の中を歩く。途中はぬかるみが多く登山靴の使用をおすすめする。また高山なので雨具は必携。愛山渓温泉へ行く分岐点（裾合平）から右に少し登った渓谷沿いにある。 ==泉質== 硫黄臭がする。泉温はかなり高くそのままでは熱すぎて入れないので、[[雪渓]]から流れ出ている渓流水を引き込んで調節する。この温度調整は[[シャベル]]または手により、湯船に流れ込む水路を土木工事して実施する。なお入湯後は肌がすべすべになる。 ==温泉街== だれかがシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外人間が作ったものは見当たらない。本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし長湯は禁物で付近には[[虻]]がたくさんいて裸の人間を見ると寄ってくる。

'''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、[[北海道]][[上川支庁]]管内[[上川郡 (石狩国)|上川郡]][[東川町]]（旧[[蝦夷地]]、[[明治]]以降の旧[[石狩国]]）にある、[[大雪山]][[旭岳]]山系のお花畑の中に湧く秘湯。 ==アクセス== [[旭川市|旭川市街]]から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で[[旭岳温泉]]まで約100分、そこから[[索道|ロープウェイ]]に乗って姿見の池に到着、その後徒歩約2時間。このアクセス道は秀麗な[[旭岳]]を右手に見ながら、[[チングルマ]]の咲き誇るお花畑の中を歩く。途中はぬかるみが多く登山靴の使用をおすすめする。また高山なので雨具は必携。[[愛山渓温泉]]へ行く分岐点（裾合平）から右に少し登った渓谷沿いにある。 ==泉質== [[硫黄|硫黄臭]]がする。泉温はかなり高くそのままでは熱すぎて入れないので、[[雪渓]]から流れ出ている渓流水を引き込んで調節する。この温度調整は[[シャベル]]または手により、湯船に流れ込む水路を土木工事して実施する。なお入湯後は肌がすべすべになる。 ==温泉街== だれかがシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外人間が作ったものは見当たらない[[野湯]]である。本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし長湯は禁物で付近には[[アブ|虻]]がたくさんいて裸の人間を見ると寄ってくる。また、[[ヒグマ]]の生息地帯でもあるため、熊除けの鈴などの持参は欠かせない。

'''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、[[北海道]][[上川支庁]]管内[[上川郡 (石狩国)|上川郡]][[東川町]]（旧[[蝦夷地]]、[[明治]]以降の旧[[石狩国]]）にある、[[大雪山]][[旭岳]]山系、[[中岳]]のお花畑の中に湧く秘湯。 ==アクセス== [[旭川市|旭川市街]]から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で[[旭岳温泉]]まで約100分、そこから[[索道|ロープウェイ]]に乗って姿見の池に到着、その後徒歩約2時間。このアクセス道は秀麗な[[旭岳]]を右手に見ながら、[[チングルマ]]の咲き誇るお花畑の中を歩く。途中はぬかるみが多く登山靴の使用をおすすめする。また高山なので雨具は必携。[[愛山渓温泉]]へ行く分岐点（裾合平）から右に少し登った渓谷沿いにある。 ==泉質== [[硫黄|硫黄臭]]がする。泉温はかなり高くそのままでは熱すぎて入れないので、[[雪渓]]から流れ出ている渓流水を引き込んで調節する。この温度調整は[[シャベル]]または手により、湯船に流れ込む水路を土木工事して実施する。なお入湯後は肌がすべすべになる。 ==温泉街== だれかがシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外人間が作ったものは見当たらない[[野湯]]である。本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし長湯は禁物で付近には[[アブ|虻]]がたくさんいて裸の人間を見ると寄ってくる。また、[[ヒグマ]]の生息地帯でもあるため、熊除けの鈴などの持参は欠かせない。
== 関連項目 == *[[温泉]]、[[温泉街]]、[[日本の温泉地一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://daisetsu.grow-web.jp/spa-naka.htm 中岳温泉]（[http://www.daisetsu.net/ 大雪山物語]）

'''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、[[北海道]][[上川支庁]]管内[[上川郡 (石狩国)|上川郡]][[東川町]]（旧[[蝦夷地]]、[[明治]]以降の旧[[石狩国]]）にある、[[大雪山]][[旭岳]]山系、[[中岳]]のお花畑の中に湧く秘湯。 [[Image:Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg|thumb|right|中岳温泉露天風呂]] ==アクセス== [[旭川市|旭川市街]]から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で[[旭岳温泉]]まで約100分、そこから[[索道|ロープウェイ]]に乗って姿見の池に到着、その後徒歩約2時間。このアクセス道は秀麗な[[旭岳]]を右手に見ながら、[[チングルマ]]の咲き誇るお花畑の中を歩く。途中の道は7月まで雪渓が残りぬかるみが続くので、登山靴の使用をおすすめする。また高山なので雨具は必携。[[愛山渓温泉]]へ行く分岐点（裾合平）から右に30分ほど登った渓谷沿いにある。 ==泉質== [[硫黄|硫黄臭]]がする。泉温はかなり高くそのままでは熱すぎて入れないので、[[雪渓]]から流れ出ている渓流水を引き込んで調節する。この温度調整は[[シャベル]]または手により、湯船に流れ込む水路を土木工事して実施する。なお入湯後は肌がすべすべになる。 [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|湯船と手入れ用のシャベル]] ==温泉街== だれかがシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外人間が作ったものは見当たらない[[野湯]]である。周辺はハイマツが茂りツガザクラの咲くお花畑で、本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし時期によっては長湯は禁物である。付近には季節によっては[[アブ|虻]]がたくさんいて、裸の人間を見ると寄ってくる。また、[[ヒグマ]]の生息地帯でもあるため、熊除けの鈴などの持参は欠かせない。

'''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、[[北海道]][[上川支庁]]管内[[上川郡 (石狩国)|上川郡]][[東川町]]（旧[[蝦夷地]]、[[明治]]以降の旧[[石狩国]]）にある、[[大雪山]][[旭岳]]山系、[[中岳]]のお花畑の中に湧く秘湯。 [[Image:Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg|thumb|right|中岳温泉露天風呂]] ==アクセス== [[旭川市|旭川市街]]から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で[[旭岳温泉]]まで約100分、そこから[[索道|ロープウェイ]]に乗って姿見の池に到着、その後徒歩約2時間。このアクセス道は秀麗な[[旭岳]]を右手に見ながら、[[チングルマ]]の咲き誇るお花畑の中を歩く。途中の道は7月まで雪渓が残りぬかるみが続くので、登山靴の使用をおすすめする。また高山なので雨具は必携。[[愛山渓温泉]]へ行く分岐点（裾合平）から右に30分ほど登った渓谷沿いにある。 ==泉質と効能== [[硫黄|硫黄臭]]がする。泉温はかなり高くそのままでは熱すぎて入れないので、[[雪渓]]から流れ出ている渓流水を引き込んで調節する。この温度調整は[[シャベル]]または手により、湯船に流れ込む水路を土木工事して実施する。湯船横の岩を脱衣置き場にできる。 [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|湯船と手入れ用のシャベル]] 山旅の疲れに特効がある。足湯だけでも気持ち良く疲労回復の効果があるが、全身浴は開放感もあって格別。なお入湯後は肌がすべすべになる。
==温泉街== だれかがシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外人間が作ったものは見当たらない[[野湯]]である。周辺はハイマツが茂りツガザクラの咲くお花畑で、本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし時期によっては長湯は禁物である。付近には季節によっては[[アブ|虻]]がたくさんいて、裸の人間を見ると寄ってくる。また、[[ヒグマ]]の生息地帯でもあるため、熊除けの鈴などの持参は欠かせない。

'''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、[[北海道]][[上川支庁]]管内[[上川郡 (石狩国)|上川郡]][[東川町]]（旧[[蝦夷地]]、[[明治]]以降の旧[[石狩国]]）にある、[[大雪山]][[旭岳]]山系、[[中岳]]の花畑の中に湧く秘湯。 [[Image:Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg|thumb|right|中岳温泉露天風呂、湯船脇の岩が脱衣置き場になる]] ==アクセス== [[Image:Daisetsu-zan in summer.jpg|thumb|right|アクセス道から見える白鳥の雪渓（2006年7月）]] [[旭川市|旭川市街]]から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で[[旭岳温泉]]まで約70分、そこから[[索道|ロープウェイ]]に乗って姿見の池に到着、その後徒歩約2時間。このアクセス道は秀麗な[[旭岳]]を右手に見ながら、[[チングルマ]]の咲き誇る花畑の中を歩く。途中の道は7月まで雪渓が残りぬかるみが続くので、登山靴の使用をおすすめする。また高山なので雨具は必携。[[愛山渓温泉]]へ行く分岐点（裾合平）から右に30分ほど登った渓谷沿いにある。 ==泉質と効能== [[硫黄|硫黄臭]]がする。泉温はかなり高くそのままでは熱すぎて入れないので、[[雪渓]]から流れ出ている渓流水を引き込んで調節する。この温度調整は[[シャベル]]または手により、湯船に流れ込む水路を土木工事して実施する。湯船横の岩を脱衣置き場にできる。 [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|湯船と手入れ用のシャベル]] 山旅の疲れに特効がある。足湯だけでも気持ち良く疲労回復の効果があるが、全身浴は開放感もあって格別。なお入湯後は肌がすべすべになる。
==温泉街== だれかがシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外人間が作ったものは見当たらない[[野湯]]である。周辺はハイマツが茂りツガザクラの咲く花畑で、本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし時期によっては長湯は禁物である。付近には季節によっては[[アブ|虻]]がたくさんいて、裸の人間を見ると寄ってくる。また、[[ヒグマ]]の生息地帯でもあるため、熊除けの鈴などの持参は欠かせない。
== 関連項目 == {{ウィキポータルリンク|温泉}} *[[温泉]]、[[温泉街]]、[[外湯]]、[[温泉番付]] *[[日本の温泉地一覧]] - [[日本の温泉画像一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.daisetsu.net/ 大雪山物語]

==温泉街== 誰かのシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外、人間が作ったものは見当たらない[[野湯]]である。周辺はハイマツが茂りツガザクラの咲く花畑で、本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし時期によっては長湯は禁物である。付近には季節によっては[[アブ|虻]]が多く発生し、人の気配に集合し裸でいることは無防備である。また、[[ヒグマ]]の生息地帯でもあるため、熊除けの鈴持参などの対策は欠かせない。

'''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、[[北海道]][[上川支庁]]管内[[上川郡 (石狩国)|上川郡]][[東川町]]（旧[[蝦夷地]]、[[明治]]以降の旧[[石狩国]]）にある、[[大雪山]][[旭岳]]山系、[[中岳]]の花畑の中に湧く[[秘湯]]。
==アクセス== [[旭川市|旭川市街]]から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で[[旭岳温泉]]まで約1時間10分、そこから[[索道|ロープウェイ]]に乗って姿見の池に下車後、徒歩約2時間。このアクセス道は裾合平と呼ばれる高原を、秀麗な[[旭岳]]を右手に見ながら、[[チングルマ]]の咲き誇る花畑の中を歩く。途中の道は7月まで雪渓が残り、ぬかるみが続くので、登山靴や長靴の使用をおすすめする。また高山なので雨具・防寒具は必携。[[愛山渓温泉]]へ行く分岐点から右に30分ほど登った渓谷沿いにある。
==泉質== [[硫黄|硫黄臭]]がする。泉温はかなり高くそのままでは熱すぎて入れないので、付近の[[雪渓]]から流れ出ている渓流水を引き込んで調節する。この温度調整は[[シャベル]]または手により、湯船に流れ込む水路を土木工事して実施する。湯船横の岩を脱衣置き場に利用できる。
=== 効能 === 山旅の疲れに効果がある。[[足湯]]だけでも気持ち良く疲労回復の効果があるが、全身浴は開放感もあって格別。入湯後は肌がすべすべになる。
''※[[効能]]はその効果を万人に保証するものではない。''
==温泉地== 誰かのシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外、人間が作ったものは見当たらない[[野湯]]である。周辺はハイマツが茂りツガザクラの咲く花畑で、本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし時期によっては長湯は禁物である。付近には季節によっては[[アブ|虻]]が多く発生し、人の気配に集合し裸でいることは無防備である。また、[[ヒグマ]]の生息地帯でもあるため、熊除けの鈴持参などの対策は欠かせない。
== 関連項目 == {{ウィキポータルリンク|温泉}} *[[温泉]]、[[温泉街]]、[[外湯]]、[[温泉番付]] *[[日本の温泉地一覧]] - [[日本の温泉画像一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.daisetsu.net/ 大雪山物語]
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかたけおんせん}} [[Category:北海道の温泉]] [[Category:大雪山]] [[Category:東川町]]

==温泉地== 誰かのシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外、人間が作ったものは見当たらない[[野湯]]である。周辺はハイマツが茂りツガザクラの咲く花畑で、本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし時期によっては長湯は禁物である。付近には季節によっては[[アブ|虻]]が多く発生し、人の気配に集合し裸でいることは無防備である。また、[[ヒグマ]]の生息地帯でもあるため、熊除けの鈴持参などの対策は欠かせない。
== 関連項目 == {{温泉}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.daisetsu.net/ 大雪山物語]
{{Coor title dms|43|40|54|N|142|51|40|E|region:JP}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかたけおんせん}} [[Category:北海道の温泉]] [[Category:大雪山]] [[Category:東川町]]

{{日本の温泉地 |名称 = 中岳温泉 |画像 = Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg |コメント = 中岳温泉露天風呂、湯船脇の岩が脱衣置き場になる |所在地 = [[北海道]][[上川郡 (石狩国)|上川郡]][[東川町]] |交通アクセス = [[旭川市]]からバスで約1時間10分。その後[[索道|ロープウェイ]]に乗って姿見の池に下車後、徒歩約2時間 |泉質 = |泉温 = |湧出量 = |pH = |液性の分類 = |浸透圧の分類 = |宿泊施設数 = |総収容人員数 = |年間浴客数 = |統計年度 = |外部リンク = |特記事項 = }}
[[Image:Daisetsu-zan in summer.jpg|thumb|right|アクセス道から見える白鳥の雪渓（2006年7月）]] [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|湯船と手入れ用のシャベル]]
'''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、[[北海道]][[上川郡 (石狩国)|上川郡]][[東川町]]にある、[[大雪山]][[旭岳]]山系、[[中岳]]の中腹に湧く[[野湯]]。
==アクセス== [[旭川市|旭川市街]]から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で[[旭岳温泉]]まで約1時間10分、そこから[[索道|ロープウェイ]]に乗って姿見の池に下車後、徒歩約2時間。このアクセス道は裾合平と呼ばれる[[高原]]を、秀麗な[[旭岳]]を右手に見ながら、[[チングルマ]]の咲き誇る[[花畑]]の中を歩く。途中の道は[[7月]]まで[[雪渓]]が残り、ぬかるみが続くので、[[登山靴]]や[[長靴]]の使用をおすすめする。また高山なので雨具・防寒具は必携。[[愛山渓温泉]]へ行く分岐点から右に30分ほど登った渓谷沿いにある。
==泉質== [[硫黄|硫黄臭]]がする。泉温はかなり高くそのままでは熱すぎて入れないので、付近の[[雪渓]]から流れ出ている渓流水を引き込んで調節する。この温度調整は[[シャベル]]または手により、湯船に流れ込む水路を土木工事して実施する。湯船横の岩を脱衣置き場に利用できる。
=== 効能 === 山旅の疲れに効果がある。[[足湯]]だけでも気持ち良く疲労回復の効果があるが、全身浴は開放感もあって格別。入湯後は肌がすべすべになる。
''※[[効能]]はその効果を万人に保証するものではない。''
==温泉地== 誰かのシャベルで掘った湯船と登山道以外、人間が作ったものは見当たらない[[野湯]]である。周辺はハイマツが茂りツガザクラの咲く花畑で、本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし時期によっては長湯は禁物である。付近には季節によっては[[アブ|虻]]が多く発生し、人の気配に集合し裸でいることは無防備である。また、[[ヒグマ]]の生息地帯でもあるため、熊除けの鈴持参などの対策は欠かせない。
== 関連項目 == {{温泉}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.daisetsu.net/ 大雪山物語] * [http://www.tabichan.jp/hotspring/notenyu/20 中岳温泉 - 旅チャンネル]
{{Coor title dms|43|40|54|N|142|51|40|E|region:JP}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかたけおんせん}} [[Category:北海道の温泉]] [[Category:大雪山]] [[Category:東川町]]

'''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、[[北海道]][[上川郡 (石狩国)|上川郡]][[東川町]]にある、[[大雪山]][[旭岳]]山系、[[中岳]]の中腹に湧く[[野湯]]。
==アクセス== [[旭川市|旭川市街]]から[[バス (交通機関)|バス]]で[[旭岳温泉]]まで約1時間10分、そこから[[索道|ロープウェイ]]に乗って姿見の池に下車後、徒歩約2時間。このアクセス道は裾合平と呼ばれる[[高原]]を、秀麗な[[旭岳]]を右手に見ながら、[[チングルマ]]の咲き誇る[[花畑]]の中を歩く。途中の道は[[7月]]まで[[雪渓]]が残り、ぬかるみが続くので、[[登山靴]]や[[長靴]]の使用をおすすめする。また高山なので雨具・防寒具は必携。[[愛山渓温泉]]へ行く分岐点から右に30分ほど登った渓谷沿いにある。
==泉質== [[硫黄|硫黄臭]]がする。泉温はかなり高くそのままでは熱すぎて入れないので、付近の[[雪渓]]から流れ出ている渓流から、[[シャベル]]などで湯道をつくり、水を引き込んで調節する。
==温泉地== 湯船横の岩を脱衣置き場に利用できる。 誰かが掘った湯船と登山道以外、人間が作ったものは見当たらない[[野湯]]である。周辺はハイマツが茂りツガザクラの咲く花畑で、本格的秘湯としてアウトドア派の注目を集めている。ただし時期によっては長湯は禁物である。付近には季節によっては[[アブ|虻]]が多く発生し、人の気配に集合し裸でいることは無防備である。また、[[ヒグマ]]の生息地帯でもあるため、熊除けの鈴持参などの対策は欠かせない。
== 関連項目 == {{温泉}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.taisetsu.net/ たいせつ★ねっと]
{{Coor title dms|43|40|54|N|142|51|40|E|region:JP}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかたけおんせん}} [[Category:北海道の温泉]] [[Category:大雪山]] [[Category:東川町]]

'''中岳温泉'''（なかだけおんせん）は、[[北海道]][[上川郡 (石狩国)|上川郡]][[東川町]]にある、[[大雪山]][[旭岳]]山系、[[中岳]]の中腹に湧く[[野湯]]。

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nakadake Onsen]][[ja:中岳温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11364339]] Wikidata name: Nakadake Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Nakadake Onsen |image = Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg |Comment = Open‐air bath at Nakadake Onsen; the rock beside the tub is used as a clothes resting spot. |Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|qq=|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}} [[:en:Higashikawa, Hokkaido|Tōkawa Town]] |Access = Approximately 1 hour 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa City]]; then take a {{ill|ropeway|ja|ロープウェイ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ropeway|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 索道|1=aerial lift|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13409250}} to disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours on foot. |Water Type = |Temperature = |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }}
[[Image:Daisetsu-zan in summer.jpg|thumb|right|Snowy, swan-like ice formations visible along the access road (July 2006)]] [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|The hot spring tub and a maintenance shovel]]
'''Nakadake Onsen''' (中岳温泉) is an undeveloped {{ill|Wild Hot Springs|lt=wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}} that bubbles up on the mid-slope of {{ill|Nakadake|ja|中岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadake|qq=|1=Nakadake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364334}} in the [[:en:Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|Daisetsuzan]] – [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] mountain range, located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|qq=|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}}, [[:en:Higashikawa, Hokkaido|Tōkawa Town]].<ref>[https://www.allthepeaks.com/post/asahidake-nakadake-onsen-loop-hokkaido Mt. Asahidake to Mt. Nakadake Onsen Loop Hike (Hokkaido)]</ref>
== Water Quality == The water has a distinct {{ill|sulfur odor|ja|硫黄臭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur odor|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The source water is extremely hot—so hot that it must be tempered before use. To adjust the temperature, water is drawn from a nearby stream running from a {{ill|snow field|ja|雪渓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/snow field|qq=|1=snow field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659916}} by constructing a hot spring channel ({{ill|Yudō|ja|湯道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudō|qq=|1=Yudō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109595339}}) using tools such as a [[:en:Shovel|shovel]].
== Onsen Area == The rock adjacent to the bathing tub serves as a makeshift changing area. Aside from the dug-out tub and hiking trails, no other man-made structures are present in this wild hot spring. The surrounding area is overgrown with [[:en:Pinus pumila|High Mats]] and adorned with blooming {{ill|Tsugazakura|ja|ツガザクラ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugazakura|qq=|1=Phyllodoce nipponica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11318710}}, attracting outdoor enthusiasts to this authentic, secret hot spring. However, prolonged bathing may be inadvisable during certain seasons. In some periods, a large number of {{ill|horseflies|ja|虻|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horseflies|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アブ|1=horseflies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17988455}} may emerge, and gathering in the open without cover is risky. In addition, since the area is a habitat for [[:en:Brown bear|brown bears]], it is essential to carry a bell or take other bear deterrence measures.
== Access == From {{ill|Asahikawa City|ja|旭川市街|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a bus to [[:en:Asahidake Onsen|Asahidake Onsen]] (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes). Then, board a ropeway and disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours of hiking. This route passes through a plateau known as Susuge-daira, offering views of the majestic [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] to the right, and traverses a flower field where {{ill|Chinguruma|ja|チングルマ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinguruma|qq=|1=Geum pentapetalum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3020977}} bloom. Alternatively, one may descend westward from the Nakadake branch along the route from {{ill|Mamiya-dake|ja|間宮岳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamiya-dake|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大雪山|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157658}} to Nakadake.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Hokkaido no Yama|author=Kenji Itō|publisher=Yama to Keikoku-sha|date=2017}}</ref>
== References == {{脚注のヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakadake Onsen]][[ja:中岳温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364339]] Wikidata name: Nakadake Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Nakadake Onsen |image = Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg |Comment = Open‐air bath at Nakadake Onsen; the rock beside the tub is used as a clothes resting spot. |Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}} [[:en:Higashikawa, Hokkaido|Tōkawa Town]] |Access = Approximately 1 hour 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa City]]; then take a {{ill|ropeway|ja|ロープウェイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ropeway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 索道|1=aerial lift|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13409250}} to disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours on foot. |Water Type = |Temperature = |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }}
[[Image:Daisetsu-zan in summer.jpg|thumb|right|Snowy, swan-like ice formations visible along the access road (July 2006)]] [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|The hot spring tub and a maintenance shovel]]
'''Nakadake Onsen''' (中岳温泉) is an undeveloped {{ill|Wild Hot Springs|lt=wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}} that bubbles up on the mid-slope of {{ill|Nakadake|ja|中岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadake|1=Nakadake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364334}} in the [[:en:Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|Daisetsuzan]] – [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] mountain range, located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}}, [[:en:Higashikawa, Hokkaido|Tōkawa Town]].<ref>[https://www.allthepeaks.com/post/asahidake-nakadake-onsen-loop-hokkaido Mt. Asahidake to Mt. Nakadake Onsen Loop Hike (Hokkaido)]</ref>
== Water Quality == The water has a distinct {{ill|sulfur odor|ja|硫黄臭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur odor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The source water is extremely hot—so hot that it must be tempered before use. To adjust the temperature, water is drawn from a nearby stream running from a {{ill|snow field|ja|雪渓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/snow field|1=snow field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659916}} by constructing a hot spring channel ({{ill|Yudō|ja|湯道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudō|1=Yudō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109595339}}) using tools such as a [[:en:Shovel|shovel]].
== Onsen Area == The rock adjacent to the bathing tub serves as a makeshift changing area. Aside from the dug-out tub and hiking trails, no other man-made structures are present in this wild hot spring. The surrounding area is overgrown with [[:en:Pinus pumila|High Mats]] and adorned with blooming {{ill|Tsugazakura|ja|ツガザクラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugazakura|1=Phyllodoce nipponica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11318710}}, attracting outdoor enthusiasts to this authentic, secret hot spring. However, prolonged bathing may be inadvisable during certain seasons. In some periods, a large number of {{ill|horseflies|ja|虻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horseflies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アブ|1=horseflies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17988455}} may emerge, and gathering in the open without cover is risky. In addition, since the area is a habitat for [[:en:Brown bear|brown bears]], it is essential to carry a bell or take other bear deterrence measures.
== Access == From {{ill|Asahikawa City|ja|旭川市街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a bus to [[:en:Asahidake Onsen|Asahidake Onsen]] (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes). Then, board a ropeway and disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours of hiking. This route passes through a plateau known as Susuge-daira, offering views of the majestic [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] to the right, and traverses a flower field where {{ill|Chinguruma|ja|チングルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinguruma|1=Geum pentapetalum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3020977}} bloom. Alternatively, one may descend westward from the Nakadake branch along the route from {{ill|Mamiya-dake|ja|間宮岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamiya-dake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大雪山|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157658}} to Nakadake.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Hokkaido no Yama|author=Kenji Itō|publisher=Yama to Keikoku-sha|date=2017}}</ref>
== References == {{脚注のヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Kenji Itō, ''Hokkaido no Yama'' (Yama to Keikoku-sha, 2017) == See Also == * [[List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.taisetsu.net/ たいせつ★ねっと] {{coord|43|40|54|N|142|51|40|E|region:JP|display=title}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakadake Onsen]][[ja:中岳温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364339]] Wikidata name: Nakadake Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Nakadake Onsen |image = Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg |Comment = Open‐air bath at Nakadake Onsen; the rock beside the tub is used as a clothes resting spot. |Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}} [[:en:Higashikawa, Hokkaido|Tōkawa Town]] |Access = Approximately 1 hour 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa City]]; then take a {{ill|ropeway|ja|ロープウェイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ropeway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 索道|1=aerial lift|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13409250}} to disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours on foot. |Water Type = |Temperature = |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }}
[[Image:Daisetsu-zan in summer.jpg|thumb|right|Snowy, swan-like ice formations visible along the access road (July 2006)]] [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|The hot spring tub and a maintenance shovel]]
'''Nakadake Onsen''' (中岳温泉) is an undeveloped {{ill|Wild Hot Springs|lt=wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}} that bubbles up on the mid-slope of {{ill|Nakadake|ja|中岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadake|1=Nakadake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364334}} in the [[:en:Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|Daisetsuzan]] – [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] mountain range, located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}}, [[:en:Higashikawa, Hokkaido|Tōkawa Town]].<ref>[https://www.allthepeaks.com/post/asahidake-nakadake-onsen-loop-hokkaido Mt. Asahidake to Mt. Nakadake Onsen Loop Hike (Hokkaido)]</ref>
== Water Quality == The water has a distinct {{ill|sulfur odor|ja|硫黄臭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur odor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The source water is extremely hot—so hot that it must be tempered before use. To adjust the temperature, water is drawn from a nearby stream running from a {{ill|snow field|ja|雪渓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/snow field|1=snow field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659916}} by constructing a hot spring channel ({{ill|Yudō|ja|湯道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudō|1=Yudō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109595339}}) using tools such as a [[:en:Shovel|shovel]].
== Onsen Area == The rock adjacent to the bathing tub serves as a makeshift changing area. Aside from the dug-out tub and hiking trails, no other man-made structures are present in this wild hot spring. The surrounding area is overgrown with [[:en:Pinus pumila|High Mats]] and adorned with blooming {{ill|Tsugazakura|ja|ツガザクラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugazakura|1=Phyllodoce nipponica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11318710}}, attracting outdoor enthusiasts to this authentic, secret hot spring. However, prolonged bathing may be inadvisable during certain seasons. In some periods, a large number of {{ill|horseflies|ja|虻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horseflies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アブ|1=horseflies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17988455}} may emerge, and gathering in the open without cover is risky. In addition, since the area is a habitat for [[:en:Brown bear|brown bears]], it is essential to carry a bell or take other bear deterrence measures.
== Access == From {{ill|Asahikawa City|ja|旭川市街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a bus to [[:en:Asahidake Onsen|Asahidake Onsen]] (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes). Then, board a ropeway and disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours of hiking. This route passes through a plateau known as Susuge-daira, offering views of the majestic [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] to the right, and traverses a flower field where {{ill|Chinguruma|ja|チングルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinguruma|1=Geum pentapetalum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3020977}} bloom. Alternatively, one may descend westward from the Nakadake branch along the route from {{ill|Mamiya-dake|ja|間宮岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamiya-dake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大雪山|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157658}} to Nakadake.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Hokkaido no Yama|author=Kenji Itō|publisher=Yama to Keikoku-sha|date=2017}}</ref>
== References == {{脚注のヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Kenji Itō, ''Hokkaido no Yama'' (Yama to Keikoku-sha, 2017) == See Also == * [[List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.taisetsu.net/ たいせつ★ねっと] {{coord|43|40|54|N|142|51|40|E|region:JP|display=title}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nakadake Onsen]][[ja:中岳温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakadake Onsen]][[ja:中岳温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364339]] Wikidata name: Nakadake Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Nakadake Onsen |image = Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg |Comment = Open‐air bath at Nakadake Onsen; the rock beside the tub is used as a clothes resting spot. |Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}} [[:en:Higashikawa, Hokkaido|Tōkawa Town]] |Access = Approximately 1 hour 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa City]]; then take a {{ill|ropeway|ja|ロープウェイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ropeway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 索道|1=aerial lift|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13409250}} to disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours on foot. |Water Type = |Temperature = |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }}
[[Image:Daisetsu-zan in summer.jpg|thumb|right|Snowy, swan-like ice formations visible along the access road (July 2006)]] [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|The hot spring tub and a maintenance shovel]]
'''Nakadake Onsen''' (中岳温泉) is an undeveloped {{ill|Wild Hot Springs|lt=wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}} that bubbles up on the mid-slope of {{ill|Nakadake|ja|中岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadake|1=Nakadake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364334}} in the [[:en:Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|Daisetsuzan]] – [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] mountain range, located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}}, [[:en:Higashikawa, Hokkaido|Tōkawa Town]].<ref>[https://www.allthepeaks.com/post/asahidake-nakadake-onsen-loop-hokkaido Mt. Asahidake to Mt. Nakadake Onsen Loop Hike (Hokkaido)]</ref>
== Water Quality == The water has a distinct {{ill|sulfur odor|ja|硫黄臭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur odor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The source water is extremely hot—so hot that it must be tempered before use. To adjust the temperature, water is drawn from a nearby stream running from a {{ill|snow field|ja|雪渓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/snow field|1=snow field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659916}} by constructing a hot spring channel ({{ill|Yudō|ja|湯道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudō|1=Yudō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109595339}}) using tools such as a [[:en:Shovel|shovel]].
== Onsen Area == The rock adjacent to the bathing tub serves as a makeshift changing area. Aside from the dug-out tub and hiking trails, no other man-made structures are present in this wild hot spring. The surrounding area is overgrown with [[:en:Pinus pumila|High Mats]] and adorned with blooming {{ill|Tsugazakura|ja|ツガザクラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugazakura|1=Phyllodoce nipponica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11318710}}, attracting outdoor enthusiasts to this authentic, secret hot spring. However, prolonged bathing may be inadvisable during certain seasons. In some periods, a large number of {{ill|horseflies|ja|虻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horseflies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アブ|1=horseflies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17988455}} may emerge, and gathering in the open without cover is risky. In addition, since the area is a habitat for [[:en:Brown bear|brown bears]], it is essential to carry a bell or take other bear deterrence measures.
== Access == From {{ill|Asahikawa City|ja|旭川市街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a bus to [[:en:Asahidake Onsen|Asahidake Onsen]] (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes). Then, board a ropeway and disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours of hiking. This route passes through a plateau known as Susuge-daira, offering views of the majestic [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] to the right, and traverses a flower field where {{ill|Chinguruma|ja|チングルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinguruma|1=Geum pentapetalum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3020977}} bloom. Alternatively, one may descend westward from the Nakadake branch along the route from {{ill|Mamiya-dake|ja|間宮岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamiya-dake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大雪山|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157658}} to Nakadake.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Hokkaido no Yama|author=Kenji Itō|publisher=Yama to Keikoku-sha|date=2017}}</ref>
== References == {{脚注のヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

In some periods, a large number of {{ill|horseflies|ja|虻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horseflies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アブ|1=horseflies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17988455}} may emerge, and gathering in the open without cover is risky. In addition, since the area is a habitat for [[:en:Brown bear|brown bears]], it is essential to carry a bell or take other bear deterrence measures.
== Access == From {{ill|Asahikawa City|ja|旭川市街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a bus to [[:en:Asahidake Onsen|Asahidake Onsen]] (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes). Then, board a ropeway and disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours of hiking. This route passes through a plateau known as Susuge-daira, offering views of the majestic [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] to the right, and traverses a flower field where {{ill|Chinguruma|ja|チングルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinguruma|1=Geum pentapetalum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3020977}} bloom. Alternatively, one may descend westward from the Nakadake branch along the route from {{ill|Mamiya-dake|ja|間宮岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamiya-dake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大雪山|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157658}} to Nakadake.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Hokkaido no Yama|author=Kenji Itō|publisher=Yama to Keikoku-sha|date=2017}}</ref>
== References == {{脚注のヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Kenji Itō, ''Hokkaido no Yama'' (Yama to Keikoku-sha, 2017) == See Also == * [[List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nakadake Onsen]][[ja:中岳温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nakadake Onsen]][[ja:中岳温泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakadake Onsen]][[ja:中岳温泉]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364339]] Wikidata name: Nakadake Onsen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Onsen |Name = Nakadake Onsen |image = Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg |Comment = Open‐air bath at Nakadake Onsen; the rock beside the tub is used as a clothes resting spot. |Location = [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]] {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}} [[:en:Higashikawa, Hokkaido|Tōkawa Town]] |Access = Approximately 1 hour 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Asahikawa|Asahikawa City]]; then take a {{ill|ropeway|ja|ロープウェイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ropeway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 索道|1=aerial lift|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13409250}} to disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours on foot. |Water Type = |Temperature = |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }}
[[Image:Daisetsu-zan in summer.jpg|thumb|right|Snowy, swan-like ice formations visible along the access road (July 2006)]] [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|The hot spring tub and a maintenance shovel]]
'''Nakadake Onsen''' (中岳温泉) is an undeveloped {{ill|Wild Hot Springs|lt=wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}} that bubbles up on the mid-slope of {{ill|Nakadake|ja|中岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadake|1=Nakadake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364334}} in the [[:en:Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|Daisetsuzan]] – [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] mountain range, located in [[:en:Hokkaido|Hokkaido]], {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}}, [[:en:Higashikawa, Hokkaido|Tōkawa Town]].<ref>[https://www.allthepeaks.com/post/asahidake-nakadake-onsen-loop-hokkaido Mt. Asahidake to Mt. Nakadake Onsen Loop Hike (Hokkaido)]</ref>
== Water Quality == The water has a distinct {{ill|sulfur odor|ja|硫黄臭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur odor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The source water is extremely hot—so hot that it must be tempered before use. To adjust the temperature, water is drawn from a nearby stream running from a {{ill|snow field|ja|雪渓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/snow field|1=snow field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659916}} by constructing a hot spring channel ({{ill|Yudō|ja|湯道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudō|1=Yudō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109595339}}) using tools such as a [[:en:Shovel|shovel]].
== Onsen Area == The rock adjacent to the bathing tub serves as a makeshift changing area. Aside from the dug-out tub and hiking trails, no other man-made structures are present in this wild hot spring. The surrounding area is overgrown with [[:en:Pinus pumila|High Mats]] and adorned with blooming {{ill|Tsugazakura|ja|ツガザクラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugazakura|1=Phyllodoce nipponica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11318710}}, attracting outdoor enthusiasts to this authentic, secret hot spring. However, prolonged bathing may be inadvisable during certain seasons. In some periods, a large number of {{ill|horseflies|ja|虻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horseflies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アブ|1=horseflies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17988455}} may emerge, and gathering in the open without cover is risky. In addition, since the area is a habitat for [[:en:Brown bear|brown bears]], it is essential to carry a bell or take other bear deterrence measures.
== Access == From {{ill|Asahikawa City|ja|旭川市街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a bus to [[:en:Asahidake Onsen|Asahidake Onsen]] (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes). This route passes through a plateau known as Susuge-daira, offering views of the majestic [[:en:Asahi-dake|Asahidake]] to the right, and traverses a flower field where {{ill|Chinguruma|ja|チングルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinguruma|1=Geum pentapetalum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3020977}} bloom. Alternatively, one may descend westward from the Nakadake branch along the route from {{ill|Mamiya-dake|ja|間宮岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamiya-dake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大雪山|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157658}} to Nakadake.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Hokkaido no Yama|author=Kenji Itō|publisher=Yama to Keikoku-sha|date=2017}}</ref>

'''Nakadake Onsen''' (中岳温泉) is an undeveloped {{ill|Wild Hot Springs|lt=wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}} that bubbles up on the mid-slope of {{ill|Nakadake|ja|中岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadake|1=Nakadake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364334}} in the {{ill|Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|en|Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|WD=Q1157658|lt=Daisetsuzan}} – {{ill|Asahi-dake|en|Asahi-dake|WD=Q2271725|lt=Asahidake}} mountain range, located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}}, {{ill|Higashikawa, Hokkaido|en|Higashikawa, Hokkaido|WD=Q1357210|lt=Tōkawa Town}}.<ref>[https://www.allthepeaks.com/post/asahidake-nakadake-onsen-loop-hokkaido Mt. Asahidake to Mt. Nakadake Onsen Loop Hike (Hokkaido)]</ref>
== Water Quality == The water has a distinct {{ill|sulfur odor|ja|硫黄臭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur odor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The source water is extremely hot—so hot that it must be tempered before use. To adjust the temperature, water is drawn from a nearby stream running from a {{ill|snow field|ja|雪渓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/snow field|1=snow field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659916}} by constructing a hot spring channel ({{ill|Yudō|ja|湯道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudō|1=Yudō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109595339}}) using tools such as a {{ill|Shovel|en|Shovel|WD=Q7220961|lt=shovel}}.
== Onsen Area == The rock adjacent to the bathing tub serves as a makeshift changing area. Aside from the dug-out tub and hiking trails, no other man-made structures are present in this wild hot spring. The surrounding area is overgrown with {{ill|Pinus pumila|en|Pinus pumila|WD=Q243964|lt=High Mats}} and adorned with blooming {{ill|Tsugazakura|ja|ツガザクラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugazakura|1=Phyllodoce nipponica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11318710}}, attracting outdoor enthusiasts to this authentic, secret hot spring. However, prolonged bathing may be inadvisable during certain seasons. In some periods, a large number of {{ill|horseflies|ja|虻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horseflies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アブ|1=horseflies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17988455}} may emerge, and gathering in the open without cover is risky. In addition, since the area is a habitat for {{ill|Brown bear|en|Brown bear|WD=Q36341|lt=brown bears}}, it is essential to carry a bell or take other bear deterrence measures.
== Access == From {{ill|Asahikawa City|ja|旭川市街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a bus to {{ill|Asahidake Onsen|en|Asahidake Onsen|WD=Q4803375|lt=Asahidake Onsen}} (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes). Then, board a ropeway and disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours of hiking. This route passes through a plateau known as Susuge-daira, offering views of the majestic {{ill|Asahi-dake|en|Asahi-dake|WD=Q2271725|lt=Asahidake}} to the right, and traverses a flower field where {{ill|Chinguruma|ja|チングルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinguruma|1=Geum pentapetalum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3020977}} bloom. Alternatively, one may descend westward from the Nakadake branch along the route from {{ill|Mamiya-dake|ja|間宮岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamiya-dake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大雪山|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157658}} to Nakadake.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Hokkaido no Yama|author=Kenji Itō|publisher=Yama to Keikoku-sha|date=2017}}</ref>
== References == {{脚注のヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Kenji Itō, ''Hokkaido no Yama'' (Yama to Keikoku-sha, 2017) == See Also == * [[List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.taisetsu.net/ たいせつ★ねっと] {{coord|43|40|54|N|142|51|40|E|region:JP|display=title}}

The surrounding area is overgrown with {{ill|Pinus pumila|en|Pinus pumila|WD=Q243964|lt=High Mats}} and adorned with blooming {{ill|Tsugazakura|ja|ツガザクラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugazakura|1=Phyllodoce nipponica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11318710}}, attracting outdoor enthusiasts to this authentic, secret hot spring. However, prolonged bathing may be inadvisable during certain seasons. In some periods, a large number of {{ill|horseflies|ja|虻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horseflies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アブ|1=horseflies|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17988455}} may emerge, and gathering in the open without cover is risky. In addition, since the area is a habitat for {{ill|Brown bear|en|Brown bear|WD=Q36341|lt=brown bears}}, it is essential to carry a bell or take other bear deterrence measures.
== Access == From {{ill|Asahikawa City|ja|旭川市街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a bus to {{ill|Asahidake Onsen|en|Asahidake Onsen|WD=Q4803375|lt=Asahidake Onsen}} (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes). Then, board a ropeway and disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours of hiking. This route passes through a plateau known as Susuge-daira, offering views of the majestic {{ill|Asahi-dake|en|Asahi-dake|WD=Q2271725|lt=Asahidake}} to the right, and traverses a flower field where {{ill|Chinguruma|ja|チングルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinguruma|1=Geum pentapetalum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3020977}} bloom. Alternatively, one may descend westward from the Nakadake branch along the route from {{ill|Mamiya-dake|ja|間宮岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamiya-dake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大雪山|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157658}} to Nakadake.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Hokkaido no Yama|author=Kenji Itō|publisher=Yama to Keikoku-sha|date=2017}}</ref>
== References == {{脚注のヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Kenji Itō, ''Hokkaido no Yama'' (Yama to Keikoku-sha, 2017) == See Also == * [[List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.taisetsu.net/ たいせつ★ねっと] {{coord|43|40|54|N|142|51|40|E|region:JP|display=title}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Nakadake Onsen |image = Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg |Comment = Open‐air bath at Nakadake Onsen; the rock beside the tub is used as a clothes resting spot. |Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}} {{ill|Higashikawa, Hokkaido|en|Higashikawa, Hokkaido|WD=Q1357210|lt=Tōkawa Town|1=Higashikawa, Hokkaido}} |Access = Approximately 1 hour 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa City|1=Asahikawa}}; then take a {{ill|ropeway|ja|ロープウェイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ropeway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 索道|1=Aerial lift|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13409250}} to disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours on foot. |Water Type = |Temperature = |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }}
[[Image:Daisetsu-zan in summer.jpg|thumb|right|Snowy, swan-like ice formations visible along the access road (July 2006)]] [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|The hot spring tub and a maintenance shovel]]
'''Nakadake Onsen''' (中岳温泉) is an undeveloped {{ill|Wild Hot Springs|lt=wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}} that bubbles up on the mid-slope of {{ill|Nakadake|ja|中岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadake|1=Nakadake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364334}} in the {{ill|Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|en|Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|WD=Q1157658|lt=Daisetsuzan|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group}} – {{ill|Asahi-dake|en|Asahi-dake|WD=Q2271725|lt=Asahidake|1=Asahi-dake}} mountain range, located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}}, {{ill|Higashikawa, Hokkaido|en|Higashikawa, Hokkaido|WD=Q1357210|lt=Tōkawa Town|1=Higashikawa, Hokkaido}}.<ref>[https://www.allthepeaks.com/post/asahidake-nakadake-onsen-loop-hokkaido Mt. Asahidake to Mt. Nakadake Onsen Loop Hike (Hokkaido)]</ref>
== Water Quality == The water has a distinct {{ill|sulfur odor|ja|硫黄臭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur odor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The source water is extremely hot—so hot that it must be tempered before use. To adjust the temperature, water is drawn from a nearby stream running from a {{ill|snow field|ja|雪渓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/snow field|1=snow field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659916}} by constructing a hot spring channel ({{ill|Yudō|ja|湯道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudō|1=Q109595339|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109595339}}) using tools such as a {{ill|Shovel|en|Shovel|WD=Q7220961|lt=shovel|1=Shovel}}.
== Onsen Area == The rock adjacent to the bathing tub serves as a makeshift changing area. Aside from the dug-out tub and hiking trails, no other man-made structures are present in this wild hot spring. The surrounding area is overgrown with {{ill|Pinus pumila|en|Pinus pumila|WD=Q243964|lt=High Mats|1=Pinus pumila}} and adorned with blooming {{ill|Tsugazakura|ja|ツガザクラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugazakura|1=Phyllodoce nipponica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11318710}}, attracting outdoor enthusiasts to this authentic, secret hot spring. However, prolonged bathing may be inadvisable during certain seasons. In some periods, a large number of {{ill|horseflies|ja|虻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horseflies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アブ|1=Q17988455|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17988455}} may emerge, and gathering in the open without cover is risky. In addition, since the area is a habitat for {{ill|Brown bear|en|Brown bear|WD=Q36341|lt=brown bears|1=Brown bear}}, it is essential to carry a bell or take other bear deterrence measures.
== Access == From {{ill|Asahikawa City|ja|旭川市街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a bus to {{ill|Asahidake Onsen|en|Asahidake Onsen|WD=Q4803375|lt=Asahidake Onsen|1=Asahidake Onsen}} (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes). Then, board a ropeway and disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours of hiking. This route passes through a plateau known as Susuge-daira, offering views of the majestic {{ill|Asahi-dake|en|Asahi-dake|WD=Q2271725|lt=Asahidake|1=Asahi-dake}} to the right, and traverses a flower field where {{ill|Chinguruma|ja|チングルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinguruma|1=Geum pentapetalum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3020977}} bloom. Alternatively, one may descend westward from the Nakadake branch along the route from {{ill|Mamiya-dake|ja|間宮岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamiya-dake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大雪山|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157658}} to Nakadake.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Hokkaido no Yama|author=Kenji Itō|publisher=Yama to Keikoku-sha|date=2017}}</ref>
== References == {{脚注のヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Kenji Itō, ''Hokkaido no Yama'' (Yama to Keikoku-sha, 2017) == See Also == * [[List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.taisetsu.net/ たいせつ★ねっと] {{coord|43|40|54|N|142|51|40|E|region:JP|display=title}}

{{infobox Onsen |Name = Nakadake Onsen |image = Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan01.jpg |Comment = Open‐air bath at Nakadake Onsen; the rock beside the tub is used as a clothes resting spot. |Location = {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}} {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}} {{ill|Higashikawa, Hokkaido|en|Higashikawa, Hokkaido|WD=Q1357210|lt=Tōkawa Town|1=Higashikawa, Hokkaido}} |Access = Approximately 1 hour 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa City|1=Asahikawa}}; then take a {{ill|ropeway|ja|ロープウェイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ropeway|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 索道|1=Aerial lift|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13409250}} to disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours on foot. |Water Type = |Temperature = |Discharge = |pH = |Liquid Classification = |Osmotic Pressure Classification = |Number of Accommodations = |Total Capacity = |Annual Bath Visitors = |Statistical Year = |External links = |Special Notes = }}
[[Image:Daisetsu-zan in summer.jpg|thumb|right|Snowy, swan-like ice formations visible along the access road (July 2006)]] [[Image:Hotsprings and maintenance-tool Nakadake hotspring daisetuzan02.jpg|thumb|right|The hot spring tub and a maintenance shovel]]
'''Nakadake Onsen''' (中岳温泉) is an undeveloped {{ill|Wild Hot Springs|lt=wild hot spring|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}} that bubbles up on the mid-slope of {{ill|Nakadake|ja|中岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakadake|1=Nakadake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11364334}} in the {{ill|Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|en|Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|WD=Q1157658|lt=Daisetsuzan|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group}} – {{ill|Asahi-dake|en|Asahi-dake|WD=Q2271725|lt=Asahidake|1=Asahi-dake}} mountain range, located in {{ill|Hokkaido|en|Hokkaido|WD=Q35581|lt=Hokkaido|1=Hokkaido}}, {{ill|Kamikawa-gun|ja|上川郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamikawa-gun|1=kamikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q863458}}, {{ill|Higashikawa, Hokkaido|en|Higashikawa, Hokkaido|WD=Q1357210|lt=Tōkawa Town|1=Higashikawa, Hokkaido}}.<ref>[https://www.allthepeaks.com/post/asahidake-nakadake-onsen-loop-hokkaido Mt. Asahidake to Mt. Nakadake Onsen Loop Hike (Hokkaido)]</ref>
== Water Quality == The water has a distinct {{ill|sulfur odor|ja|硫黄臭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sulfur odor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The source water is extremely hot—so hot that it must be tempered before use. To adjust the temperature, water is drawn from a nearby stream running from a {{ill|snow field|ja|雪渓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/snow field|1=snow field|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659916}} by constructing a hot spring channel ({{ill|Yudō|ja|湯道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yudō|1=Q109595339|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109595339}}) using tools such as a {{ill|Shovel|en|Shovel|WD=Q7220961|lt=shovel|1=Shovel}}.
== Onsen Area == The rock adjacent to the bathing tub serves as a makeshift changing area. Aside from the dug-out tub and hiking trails, no other man-made structures are present in this wild hot spring. The surrounding area is overgrown with {{ill|Pinus pumila|en|Pinus pumila|WD=Q243964|lt=High Mats|1=Pinus pumila}} and adorned with blooming {{ill|Tsugazakura|ja|ツガザクラ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugazakura|1=Phyllodoce nipponica|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11318710}}, attracting outdoor enthusiasts to this authentic, secret hot spring. However, prolonged bathing may be inadvisable during certain seasons. In some periods, a large number of {{ill|horseflies|ja|虻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horseflies|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アブ|1=Q17988455|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17988455}} may emerge, and gathering in the open without cover is risky. In addition, since the area is a habitat for {{ill|Brown bear|en|Brown bear|WD=Q36341|lt=brown bears|1=Brown bear}}, it is essential to carry a bell or take other bear deterrence measures.
== Access == From {{ill|Asahikawa City|ja|旭川市街|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahikawa City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, take a bus to {{ill|Asahidake Onsen|en|Asahidake Onsen|WD=Q4803375|lt=Asahidake Onsen|1=Asahidake Onsen}} (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes). Then, board a ropeway and disembark at Sugami-no-ike, followed by about 2 hours of hiking. This route passes through a plateau known as Susuge-daira, offering views of the majestic {{ill|Asahi-dake|en|Asahi-dake|WD=Q2271725|lt=Asahidake|1=Asahi-dake}} to the right, and traverses a flower field where {{ill|Chinguruma|ja|チングルマ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinguruma|1=Geum pentapetalum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3020977}} bloom. Alternatively, one may descend westward from the Nakadake branch along the route from {{ill|Mamiya-dake|ja|間宮岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mamiya-dake|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大雪山|1=Daisetsuzan Volcanic Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1157658}} to Nakadake.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Hokkaido no Yama|author=Kenji Itō|publisher=Yama to Keikoku-sha|date=2017}}</ref>
== References == {{脚注のヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Kenji Itō, ''Hokkaido no Yama'' (Yama to Keikoku-sha, 2017) == See Also == * [[List of Hot Spring Resorts in Japan]]
== External Links == * [http://www.taisetsu.net/ たいせつ★ねっと] {{coord|43|40|54|N|142|51|40|E|region:JP|display=title}}
